overview: This green pauper infect deck leverages low-cost creatures and equipment to rapidly accumulate poison counters. By equipping Rancor to threats like Ichorclaw Myr, you create unblockable attackers that bypass traditional defense. Mutagenic Growth serves as a versatile tool to clear blockers, draw cards, and push lethal damage when needed.
win_conditions: Deal 10 poison counters to an opponent through combat damage
Flood the board with unblockable infect creatures to overwhelm blockers
Cast Mutagenic Growth on your creatures to deal direct poison damage and draw into finishing resources

keep_criteria: Hands containing one or two one-drop infect creatures
Hands with Rancor and a creature to equip
Hands with at least two land cards for consistent mana flow
Keep hands with 1-2 lands and a turn-1 creature (Kithkin, Ichorclaw Myr) or a creature paired with a pump spell (Mutagenic Growth, Rancor).
Keep any hand with two pump spells.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero lands or four or more lands
Hands lacking early infect threats or Rancor
Hands composed entirely of high-cost cards
Mulligan pure land hands, pump-only hands, or 3+ land hands with no early threats.

early_game: Prioritize land drops to ensure consistent mana availability
Play Ichorclaw Myr or similar one-drops to establish early board presence
Attach Rancor immediately to create an unblockable threat
If you have a pump spell, cast it on your attacker.
Turn 2: Play land, cast a second creature or pump spell, and attack with your pumped threat.
Attach Rancor to attackers when possible.
Prioritize land drops to maintain curve and ensure you can cast threats and pumps on time.
mid_game: Push poison damage every turn using trample and infect
Cast Mutagenic Growth on opponent's blockers to clear the path while drawing cards
Maintain board pressure to prevent the opponent from stabilizing
always preserve enough for both threats and pumps.
Do not overextend without pump support.
Keep pump spells flexible to close out games or save creatures from targeted removal.
Track your hand size carefully, as Infect often wins by turn 4-5.
Commit threats when you have the mana to pump them or when the opponent lacks removal.
late_game: Flood the board with multiple infect creatures to exhaust opponent's removal
Use Mutagenic Growth as a finisher to deal direct damage and refill your hand
Leverage excess mana to equip multiple Rancors or cast additional threats
Pump spells (Mutagenic Growth, Rancor) combined with creatures to deal 21 poison.
Ichorclaw Myr can be pumped to lethal.
Attack with a 3+ power creature or chain pumps to close out.
Calculate poison damage carefully, factoring in blockers and removal.
Go all-in when you have enough mana to pump your attacker to lethal poison, or when the opponent has no removal in hand or on the board.
If you are at 20 life and they have no threats, commit fully to close out the game.

key_cards.name: Mutagenic Growth
key_cards.notes: Cast on your infect creatures to deal poison damage equal to their power and draw cards, or cast on opponent's creatures to remove threats while gaining value.

key_cards.name: Rancor
key_cards.notes: Attach to any infect creature to give +1/+1 and trample, ensuring it deals combat damage and pushes poison counters efficiently.

key_cards.name: Ichorclaw Myr
key_cards.notes: Provides a cheap, flying infect body that can be equipped with Rancor to apply immediate pressure and bypass early blockers.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting Rancor before attacking to avoid opponent interaction
Cast Mutagenic Growth after blockers are declared to maximize card draw and damage
Hold up mana for instant-speed responses if possible, though this deck is primarily sorcery-speed
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the board without enough pump spells to close out.
Remember that Ichorclaw Myr is an artifact, so it can be targeted by artifact hate, but it also synergizes well with pump spells.
Rancor is an enchantment, so it can be destroyed by enchantment removal.
Mutagenic Growth is instant, so you can use it in response to removal to save your creature or close out.
Keep an eye on the opponent's life total and poison counters to know exactly when to go for lethal.

matchups.advice: Race quickly by attaching Rancor to your largest threat and pushing poison every turn
Use Mutagenic Growth to draw into answers and apply steady pressure with multiple small infect creatures
Establish an early board presence with Ichorclaw Myr and Rancor before they can develop their game plan

Use cheap, efficient threats that can be cast in response to counterspells if needed. Rely on artifact creatures and instant-speed pumps to close out before they can counter key threats. Avoid overcommitting to expensive spells that can be countered.

Race them relentlessly. Do not let them stabilize. Use pump spells to close out before their mana advantage translates to board presence. If they deploy a big threat, pump your creature to trade efficiently or kill it before it attacks.

It is a race. Prioritize drawing pump spells and creatures. Track both players' life totals and poison counters. Use your pump spells to close out before they do. If you have a removal spell, use it to clear their threat before attacking.

common_threats: Watch for targeted removal (Lightning Bolt, Go for the Throat) and board wipes.
Note land destruction or discard effects.
Track the opponent's life total and poison counters meticulously to know exactly when you are at lethal range.
